claims.claim: Excited about AI × crypto convergence (agents transacting, machine-to-machine payments, proof of personhood, verifiable compute)
claims.label: confirmed

claims.claim: Excited about DePIN (decentralized physical infrastructure networks)
claims.label: confirmed

claims.claim: Excited about stablecoins & payments as settlement layer
claims.label: confirmed

claims.claim: Concerned about centralization of AI (closed platforms concentrating power)
claims.label: confirmed

claims.claim: Risk-on, decade-arc constructive conviction (building through volatility)
claims.label: confirmed

missed_changes: Made privacy the central, repeatedly-stressed thesis — 'privacy is the only moat,' privacy as lock-in/network effect, programmable privacy for institutions (ZK/MPC/TEE/FHE); not anticipated in the prediction
Newly took a vocal pro-regulation advocacy stance (GENIUS Act praise, repeated calls to pass the CLARITY bill)
Developed an explicit token value-accrual / network-effects argument defending that tokens compound as claims on protocol cash flows

Articulated a sequenced consumer-crypto thesis (finance first, then gaming/social once hundreds of millions are onchain) and named onchain capital markets — perps, prediction markets, lending — as emerging strengths
Emphasized credible neutrality / decentralization-for-shared-infrastructure as the core value framing in debates
notes: Every sector and risk claim in the prediction is directly confirmed by the window, most heavily (stablecoins, AI×crypto, the 'fundamentals at all-time highs vs sentiment at lows' anti-speculation framing, and the $2.2B Fund 5 raise as a clear risk-on act). DePIN and verifiable compute are confirmed more lightly (Fund 5 essay listing decentralized infrastructure and onchain compute/energy/credit markets, Jolt zkVM, congratulating Helium's founder). The token arrays were empty and the person stayed largely at the sector level, though he did defend token value accrual generically (SOL/ETH) without making liquid-token calls. The biggest unanticipated development is the dominant focus on privacy as the defensibility/moat thesis.
